secondary_archetype: Generalist PM with developer tools focus

level_signals: 5+ years of product management experience required
Title explicitly states Senior Product Manager
Expected to own product strategy and drive user experience vision

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience with a track record of building exceptional user experiences
Strong product sense and intuition for what makes great developer tools and experiences
Experience conducting user research and translating insights into product strategy
Deep intuition about AI trends and products, particularly in automation and natural language interfaces
Excellent communication skills and ability to influence cross-functional teams
Obsessed with creating exceptional user experiences and have a track record of building beloved products
Strong at qualitative and quantitative analysis, turning user insights into actionable product decisions

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You're an active Replit user who understands the developer community
You've built or contributed to developer tools that others love using
You have experience teaching or mentoring other developers
You've worked at companies with strong product culture and user-centric design

scope.surface: AI-powered development tools and user experience for Replit's agentic software creation platform
scope.business_model: developer-facing platform, B2C with potential B2B (SaaS/freemium implied)
primary_metrics_inferred: AI feature adoption across skill levels
core_responsibilities: Define and execute the user experience vision for AI-driven software creation
Conduct deep user research to understand developer pain points and workflow needs
Design AI-powered features that feel intuitive and magical to users of all skill levels
Own user journey optimization from onboarding through advanced AI-assisted development
Drive product strategy based on user feedback, market research, and competitive analysis
Partner with design teams to create compelling AI interaction patterns and interfaces

compensation.equity_form: equity (stock options or RSUs implied by 'Competitive Salary & Equity')

green_flags: Clear and specific product surface (AI-powered dev tools, agentic platform)
Encourages applications from underrepresented and non-traditional backgrounds
Strong benefit package including wellness stipend, parental leave, and flexible PTO
red_flags: Location type is entirely unclear — no city, remote policy, or office requirement mentioned in JD
JD is light on specifics around team size, reporting structure, and exact ownership boundaries

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title and 5+ years requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and 'millions of users worldwide')
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.type (JD does not specify remote, hybrid, or onsite; marked unclear)
compensation.equity_form (inferred from 'Competitive Salary & Equity'; form not specified)
ambiguities: Location is entirely unspecified in the JD body — no city, no remote/hybrid/onsite designation. Benefits mention 'Commuter Benefits' and 'In Office Amenities' suggesting some in-office presence, but no formal policy is stated.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not mentioned anywhere in the JD.
